---
title: "Random Forest - Full Models (Demographics & Geography)"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---


# =====================================================================
# 1. Setup and Data Loading
# =====================================================================
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load all necessary libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(PRROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)

# Read the correct dataset containing geographic variables to avoid missing column errors
data_full <- read.csv("data/nsduh_clean_with_indexes_geo.csv")

# Define all predictors (Base + Demographics + Geography)
all_predictors <- c(
  "EDUHIGHCAT", "ECONOMIC_STATUS_INDEX", "EMPLOYMENT_INDEX", 
  "ECONOMIC_ASSISTANCE_INDEX", "MENTAL_HEALTH_INDEX", "IRINSUR4", # Base Predictors
  "CATAG3", "IRSEX", "IRMARIT",                                    # Demographic Predictors
  "COUTYP4", "PDEN10"                                             # Geographic Predictors
)
```


# =====================================================================
# 2. Model Training (Full Models)
# =====================================================================
```{r}
# --- MODEL 1: HEROIN USERS ONLY (Down-sampled / Balanced) ---
rf_heroin_full <- data_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y_HEROIN)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Y_HEROIN, all_of(all_predictors)) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(across(c(Y_HEROIN, CATAG3, IRSEX, IRMARIT, COUTYP4, PDEN10), as.factor))

min_class_heroin <- min(table(rf_heroin_full$Y_HEROIN))

set.seed(42) # Set seed for reproducibility
model_heroin <- randomForest(
  Y_HEROIN ~ ., 
  data = rf_heroin_full,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  sampsize = c(min_class_heroin, min_class_heroin) # Balance classes
)

# --- MODEL 2: HEROIN + COCAINE (Down-sampled / Balanced) ---
rf_hc_full <- data_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y_HEROIN) | !is.na(Y_COCAINE)) %>%
  mutate(Y_MODEL = ifelse(Y_HEROIN == 1 | Y_COCAINE == 1, 1, 0)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Y_MODEL, all_of(all_predictors)) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(across(c(Y_MODEL, CATAG3, IRSEX, IRMARIT, COUTYP4, PDEN10), as.factor))

min_class_hc <- min(table(rf_hc_full$Y_MODEL))

set.seed(42)
model_hc <- randomForest(
  Y_MODEL ~ ., 
  data = rf_hc_full,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  sampsize = c(min_class_hc, min_class_hc) # Balance classes
)

# --- MODEL 3: ALL DRUGS (Natural Sampling / No sampsize) ---
rf_all_full <- data_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y_HEROIN) | !is.na(Y_COCAINE) | !is.na(Y_METH)) %>%
  mutate(Y_MODEL = ifelse(Y_HEROIN == 1 | Y_COCAINE == 1 | Y_METH == 1, 1, 0)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Y_MODEL, all_of(all_predictors)) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(across(c(Y_MODEL, CATAG3, IRSEX, IRMARIT, COUTYP4, PDEN10), as.factor))

set.seed(42)
model_all_natural <- randomForest(
  Y_MODEL ~ ., 
  data = rf_all_full,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE # Keeps the natural distribution of data
)
```


# =====================================================================
# 3. Model Evaluation and Comparison
# =====================================================================
```{r}
# Helper function to calculate PR-AUC from model probabilities and true labels
get_pr_auc <- function(model, true_labels) {
  probs <- predict(model, type = "prob")[, "1"]
  fg <- probs[true_labels == "1"] # Probabilities for actual positive cases
  bg <- probs[true_labels == "0"] # Probabilities for actual negative cases
  pr <- pr.curve(scores.class0 = fg, scores.class1 = bg, curve = FALSE)
  return(pr$auc.integral)
}

# --- A. Metrics for Heroin Only Full Model ---
pred_heroin  <- predict(model_heroin)
cm_heroin    <- confusionMatrix(pred_heroin, rf_heroin_full$Y_HEROIN, positive = "1")
prob_heroin  <- predict(model_heroin, type = "prob")[, "1"]
auc_heroin   <- auc(roc(rf_heroin_full$Y_HEROIN, prob_heroin, quiet = TRUE))
prauc_heroin <- get_pr_auc(model_heroin, rf_heroin_full$Y_HEROIN)

# --- B. Metrics for Heroin + Cocaine Full Model ---
pred_hc  <- predict(model_hc)
cm_hc    <- confusionMatrix(pred_hc, rf_hc_full$Y_MODEL, positive = "1")
prob_hc  <- predict(model_hc, type = "prob")[, "1"]
auc_hc   <- auc(roc(rf_hc_full$Y_MODEL, prob_hc, quiet = TRUE))
prauc_hc <- get_pr_auc(model_hc, rf_hc_full$Y_MODEL)

# --- C. Metrics for All Drugs Natural Model ---
pred_all  <- predict(model_all_natural)
cm_all    <- confusionMatrix(pred_all, rf_all_full$Y_MODEL, positive = "1")
prob_all  <- predict(model_all_natural, type = "prob")[, "1"]
auc_all   <- auc(roc(rf_all_full$Y_MODEL, prob_all, quiet = TRUE))
prauc_all <- get_pr_auc(model_all_natural, rf_all_full$Y_MODEL)

# --- Build the comprehensive metrics table ---
final_metrics_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("1. Heroin Only", "2. Heroin + Cocaine", "3. All Drugs (Natural)"),
  Accuracy = c(cm_heroin$overall["Accuracy"], cm_hc$overall["Accuracy"], cm_all$overall["Accuracy"]),
  Balanced_Accuracy = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"], cm_hc$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"], cm_all$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]),
  Sensitivity_Recall = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Sensitivity"], cm_hc$byClass["Sensitivity"], cm_all$byClass["Sensitivity"]),
  Specificity = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Specificity"], cm_hc$byClass["Specificity"], cm_all$byClass["Specificity"]),
  Precision = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Pos Pred Value"], cm_hc$byClass["Pos Pred Value"], cm_all$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]),
  F1_Score = c(cm_heroin$byClass["F1"], cm_hc$byClass["F1"], cm_all$byClass["F1"]),
  ROC_AUC = c(auc_heroin, auc_hc, auc_all),
  PR_AUC = c(prauc_heroin, prauc_hc, prauc_all)
)

# Clean formatting and print with 3 decimal digits
rownames(final_metrics_table) <- NULL
final_metrics_table[,-1] <- round(final_metrics_table[,-1], 3)

cat("\n=========================================================================\n")
cat("          Final Extended Metrics Table - Full Models Comparison          \n")
cat("=========================================================================\n\n")
print(final_metrics_table)
```

# =====================================================================
# 4. Visualizations & Advanced Diagnostic Plots
# =====================================================================
```{r}
# --- A. Feature Importance Plots for all 3 models ---
layout(matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 3), 2, 2, byrow = TRUE))
varImpPlot(model_heroin, main = "Feature Importance: Heroin Only", pch = 19, color = "darkblue")
varImpPlot(model_hc, main = "Feature Importance: Heroin + Cocaine", pch = 19, color = "darkgreen")
varImpPlot(model_all_natural, main = "Feature Importance: All Drugs (Natural)", pch = 19, color = "darkred")
layout(1) # Reset layout back to default
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Extract importance data from the trained model
importance_data <- importance(model_all_natural)

# 2. Create a data frame for visualization (using MeanDecreaseGini)
importance_df <- data.frame(Variable = rownames(importance_data),
                            Value = importance_data[, "MeanDecreaseGini"])

# 3. Sort by importance value
importance_df <- importance_df %>% 
  arrange(desc(Value))

# 4. Create an aesthetic and clear Lollipop chart
ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Value), y = Value)) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = Variable, yend = 0), color = "gray50", linewidth = 1) + 
  geom_point(size = 5, color = "darkred") + 
  coord_flip() + # Flips axes for better readability of variable names
  labs(title = "Feature Importance: All Drugs (Natural)",
       subtitle = "Metric: Mean Decrease Gini",
       x = "Variable",
       y = "Importance Score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```



```{r}
# --- B. Confusion Matrix Heatmap (All Drugs Natural Model) ---
cm_table <- table(Predicted = model_all_natural$predicted, Actual = rf_all_full$Y_MODEL)
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_table)

ggplot(data = cm_df, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 8, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#56B1F7", high = "#132B43") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix: All Drugs (Natural Model)",
       x = "Actual Class (0 = Clean, 1 = User)",
       y = "Predicted Class") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
# --- C. ROC Curve (All Drugs Natural Model) ---
roc_natural <- roc(rf_all_full$Y_MODEL, prob_all, quiet = TRUE)
auc_natural <- round(auc(roc_natural), 3)

ggroc(roc_natural, colour = "darkred", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 1, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.25, y = 0.25, label = paste("AUC =", auc_natural), 
           size = 6, fontface = "bold", color = "darkred") +
  labs(title = "ROC Curve: All Drugs (Natural Model)",
       x = "Specificity (True Negative Rate)",
       y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# --- D. Partial Dependence Plot (Mental Health Index on All Drugs Model) ---
# Visualizes the marginal effect of mental health on the log-odds of drug use
partialPlot(x = model_all_natural, 
            pred.data = as.data.frame(rf_all_full), 
            x.var = "MENTAL_HEALTH_INDEX", 
            which.class = "1", # Probability of being a user
            main = "Partial Dependence: Mental Health Index (Full Model)",
            xlab = "Mental Health Index (Normalized)",
            ylab = "Log-odds of Continued Drug Use",
            lwd = 3, col = "darkred")
```

```{r}
# --- E. Surrogate Decision Tree Visualization ---
# Provides an interpretable view of the data structure using a single shallow tree
surrogate_tree <- rpart(Y_MODEL ~ ., 
                        data = rf_all_full, 
                        method = "class", 
                        control = rpart.control(maxdepth = 3, cp = 0.01))

rpart.plot(surrogate_tree, 
           type = 3, extra = 106, under = TRUE, faclen = 0, cex = 0.8,
           main = "Representative Decision Tree (Surrogate Model - Full Variables)",
           box.palette = c("lightblue", "lightcoral"))

```
